sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins and 3 losses, with a notable 3-0 victory against Club Portugalete but struggling against stronger La Liga sides. Real Sociedad, meanwhile, show better form with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss in their last 5, displaying attacking intent with an average of over 2 goals per game recently. Their ability to score in crucial moments gives them a slight edge coming into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, both teams have claimed a victory each, with Alavés winning 1-0 at home and Sociedad falling 1-2 at home earlier this season. Matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in most recent fixtures, suggesting a tight contest today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés, sitting 14th with 15 points, struggle offensively, ranking 18th in goals scored (12), though their defense is relatively solid (5th fewest goals conceded). Real Sociedad, in 10th with 16 points, have a more balanced attack (19 goals scored) but have conceded 21 goals, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Sociedad’s superior big chance creation (top 10) could be key against Alavés’ weaker attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Alavés will rely on players like Carlos Vicente (3 goals) to spark their attack, but the absence of Antonio Blanco in midfield may hurt their control. Sociedad miss key forward Mikel Oyarzabal (5 goals), though players like Ander Barrenetxea (3 goals, 3 assists) and Brais Méndez (3 goals) remain threats. Sociedad’s depth in attack could tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (25% home win, 33% draw, 42% away win) and Real Sociedad’s better recent form and attacking output, they are slight favorites. However, Alavés’ home advantage at Estadio de Mendizorroza and their defensive resilience could make it a close game. Expect a low-scoring affair with Sociedad likely to edge it late.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Deportivo Alavés leads Real Sociedad by a narrow margin of 1-0 at the Estadio de Mendizorroza. The home side managed to secure their advantage through a penalty converted by Lucas Boyé right at the end of the first half. While the scoreline suggests a tight contest, the statistics and momentum shifts indicate that Alavés has had the upper hand in creating meaningful opportunities. Real Sociedad, despite holding a slight edge in ball possession, struggled to convert their chances into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, there were notable shifts in attacking momentum between the two sides. Alavés started with some early intent but saw Real Sociedad take control with a strong spell of pressure shortly after. However, the tide turned back in favor of Alavés with sustained attacking spells around the midway point of the half. Sociedad fought back with several bursts of momentum later on, pushing forward with intent, but Alavés regained dominance towards the closing stages, culminating in the crucial penalty goal. These fluctuations suggest both teams are capable of turning the game in their favor, but Alavés appears more clinical in key moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the half-time stats, Deportivo Alavés has shown greater threat in the final third, creating more big chances and registering a significantly higher expected goals figure compared to Real Sociedad. Their entries into the penalty area and possessions won in dangerous zones highlight their effectiveness in attack. On the other hand, Real Sociedad has dominated in terms of duels and aerial battles, showcasing physical superiority. However, their inability to capitalize on chances, including hitting the woodwork, has left them trailing. Possession is almost evenly split, indicating a balanced contest in midfield.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout performers
halftimeSections.text: For Alavés, Lucas Boyé has been the standout player, not only for scoring the decisive penalty but also for his involvement in creating opportunities despite missing a big chance earlier. Abderrahman Rebbach also contributed significantly by creating a big chance for his team. On Real Sociedad’s side, Sergio Gómez has been a creative force with key passes and a big chance created, while Carlos Soler showed intent with a shot on target but missed a key opportunity. These players will likely be pivotal in the second half as both teams push for a result.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Alavés seems to be focusing on exploiting the final third with direct entries and quick transitions, which has paid off with their penalty goal. Their defensive structure has held firm against Sociedad’s physical approach, though they’ve struggled in duels. Real Sociedad, meanwhile, appears to prioritize control through possession and winning individual battles, but their finishing has let them down. Disciplinary issues could also be a concern for Sociedad, having picked up two yellow cards already, which might affect their aggression in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, Real Sociedad will likely come out with renewed energy to level the score, given their physical dominance and ability to create chances. They’ll need to be more clinical in front of goal to overcome Alavés’ resilient defense. On the other hand, Alavés will aim to capitalize on counter-attacks and maintain their lead, potentially looking for Boyé to add to his tally. The game remains open, but Alavés’ efficiency in attack gives them a slight edge to hold or extend their advantage, while Sociedad’s desperation for an equalizer could expose them at the back.
